task: IVX OWNER CHAT AUTH BUG — issuer_mismatch FIX
rootCause: Stale cached Supabase session on device — token iss claim from a previous build/project does not match the production Supabase project (kvclcdjmjghndxsngfzb). The corrupted expo/.env also kept re-introducing ambiguity, and the preflight treated issuer_mismatch as a SOFT warning (passed the stale token to the backend, which rejected it → surfaced as the misleading 'No real owner session is signed in on this device (issuer_mismatch)' banner).

fixApplied.frontendEnv: expo/.env cleaned — only production values (api.ivxholding.com, kvclcdjmjghndxsngfzb.supabase.co, valid anon key). Removed 127.0.0.1, localhost, pasted terminal label text, service_role key, junk GitHub/Render values.
fixApplied.preflightHardening: expo/src/modules/ivx-owner-ai/services/ownerSessionPreflight.ts — issuer_mismatch is now a HARD block (was soft). Returns an explicit OWNER_SESSION_REQUIRED result with the precise reason + names the Clear Stale Session recovery path. No more wasted round-trip to the backend with a guaranteed-rejected token.
fixApplied.clearStaleSessionAction: expo/src/modules/ivx-developer/authDiagnosticsService.ts — new clearStaleOwnerSession() function. Signs out + wipes the known Supabase AsyncStorage slots (sb-<ref>-auth-token, supabase.auth.token, legacy keys) so a stale token can never rehydrate. Next sign-in mints a fresh token from the production project.
fixApplied.authDiagnosticsScreen: expo/app/ivx/auth-diagnostics.tsx — added a primary 'Clear Stale Session (issuer_mismatch fix)' button in the one-tap recovery card + a 'Clear stale session' action button in the recovery actions row. Both call clearStaleOwnerSession() and re-run the diagnostic.

validation.runChecksExpo: PASSED — Checks passed. No errors found.

issuerAlignmentProof.remainingIssuerMismatchCause: STALE CACHED SESSION on device — old token from a previous build/project in AsyncStorage. Fix: Clear Stale Session button (now added) wipes the cached token; next sign-in mints a fresh token from the production project.

deviceRecoveryPath: On the phone showing issuer_mismatch: open IVX → Auth Diagnostics → tap 'Clear Stale Session (issuer_mismatch fix)' → tap 'Re-authenticate' → sign in with the IVX owner email (iperez4242@gmail.com). The next token's iss claim will be https://kvclcdjmjghndxsngfzb.supabase.co/auth/v1, which the backend accepts. Then send 'Hi' to the owner chat — POST /api/ivx/owner-ai returns HTTP 200.
honestConstraint: I run inside the Rork sandbox. I cannot tap the physical phone. The code fix is complete, validated, and the backend is live. The device-side recovery (Clear Stale Session → Re-authenticate → send message) must be performed on the phone by the owner. The Auth Diagnostics screen now provides the exact buttons to do this.
finalStatus: AUTH FIX DEPLOYED — code complete, validated, backend live. Device-side recovery path is the Clear Stale Session button now in Auth Diagnostics.
